inspection_findings.row_major_block_table_is_intentional: The _batch_full_spans implementation explicitly writes physical slot ids as row-relative block-table deltas (slot-row)*blocks, and the HIP context kernel then adds the active row base. This matches the row-major full KV cache shape, so changing that arithmetic would be speculative and unsafe.
inspection_findings.rowchunk_semantics: Rowchunking layer3 does not switch to a different context kernel; it slices hidden/slots/positions and calls the same native full-attention batch layer with tokens=chunk_rows plus row-aware GEMV O output. Therefore the difference under test is active row group size/row semantics, not a separate kernel family.
inspection_findings.hidden_bisect_result: For layer limit 4 and decode trace window 80..105, both current rowchunk(3,15) and layer3-native/layer15-rowchunk paths are hidden_passed=true and token_passed=true at hidden_atol=1e-3. Full-attention stage traces show only fp16-bit mlp_input drift under tolerance, with input/output/stage checks otherwise passed.
inspection_findings.blocker_refinement: Iter480 proved the full-model layer3-native path diverges at generated-token row0@82, but iter481 shows no immediate >atol hidden/token failure through layer4. The remaining blocker is likely later-layer amplification of sub-ulp/bit drift or a row-group-size-sensitive stage beyond layer4, not an obviously wrong row-major KV/cache indexing bug.
inspection_findings.next_target: Avoid a speculative kernel row-index edit. Use a narrower later-layer hidden-bisect window or add cheap first-failing-layer search support before attempting a native layer3 fix; keep c4 auto rowchunk layers at 3,15.

prompt_verifier.evidence: PASS: focused hidden/full-attention parity evidence shows both the current c4 rowchunk path and the layer3-native diagnostic are hidden/token-green through layer4 in the known divergence window, eliminating immediate layer3 >atol hidden/context/O failure and refining the blocker to later-layer amplification/row-group semantics. No performance claim.

conclusion: No code change was made: source inspection found the suspected row-major block-table arithmetic intentional, and hidden-bisect eliminated immediate layer3 >atol hidden/token failure through layer4. Keep c4 default rowchunk layers at 3,15 and target later-layer first-failure localization next.

hidden_bisect_overview.note: Current green c4 default: rowchunk layer3 and layer15, layer limit 4, trace decode window 80..105.
Diagnostic layer3-native path: only layer15 rowchunked, layer limit 4, same trace window.

source_inspection_overview.label: _batch_full_spans encodes row-relative block table offsets intentionally
rowchunk branch slices rows/slots/positions before span construction
rowchunk branch calls the same full-attention batch layer with smaller token count and row-aware O path
batch context/gate wrapper is explicitly the retained short-context decode path
batch paged-attention context kernel row/head indexing header
batch paged-attention context kernel reads row-relative block table with row-major cache addressing
